macro_architecture.reasoning: 2 active containers (backend, frontend) with 951 total components. Backend uses Layered pattern, Frontend uses Component-Based pattern.

container_analyses.summary: The backend follows a classic layered architecture built on Spring Boot, exposing REST APIs for a rich legal document and deed management domain. It implements a broad set of capabilities—from CRUD actions and key‑management to archiving and scheduled jobs—while integrating with relational databases, external KMS, and authorization services. Quality analysis shows moderate separation of concerns, coupling, and cohesion, with a C grade reflecting architectural debt such as thin or God controllers/services and an anemic domain model. Targeted refactoring toward clearer service interfaces, stronger domain‑driven design, and tighter layer boundaries will raise maintainability and scalability.

capabilities: Create, read, update, and delete actions
Persist and manage deed entries, including connections, logs and locks
Handle document metadata storage and retrieval
Perform handover data‑set management and finalization
Execute archiving operations and sign submission/re‑encryption tokens
Manage cryptographic keys, re‑encryption state and key‑manager queries
Provide business‑purpose lookup and validation services
Generate reports and export data
Schedule and run background jobs (e.g., re‑encryption, cleanup)
Expose REST APIs for all domain operations
Perform health‑check monitoring
Integrate with external authentication/authorization services via JSON auth
Apply correction notes and change tracking
functional.bounded_context: Legal Document & Deed Management with Archiving and Cryptographic Key Handling

debt_indicators: Large number of controllers and services (potentially thin or God controllers/services)
High entity count suggests possible anemic domain model and tight coupling to persistence
Missing clear service interfaces for many implementations
Potential business logic leakage into controller layer
recommendations: Introduce service interfaces and depend on abstractions rather than concrete implementations
Consolidate related controllers into cohesive modules or group by bounded context
Refactor oversized services into smaller, focused components
Apply domain‑driven design principles to move business logic into domain layer and reduce anemic entities
Add architectural guidelines to enforce layer boundaries and reduce cross‑layer dependencies

container_analyses.overall_assessment: The backend is functionally comprehensive and technically aligned with modern Spring‑based practices, but architectural quality suffers from moderate coupling and anemic domain modeling. The current design permits rapid feature addition but hampers long‑term maintainability. Addressing the identified debt—especially by introducing clear abstractions, tightening layer boundaries, and enriching the domain layer—will significantly improve cohesion, reduce coupling, and elevate the overall grade.
top_recommendations: Introduce service interfaces and depend on abstractions rather than concrete implementations
Apply domain‑driven design principles to move business logic into the domain layer and reduce anemic entities
Refactor oversized services into smaller, focused components

container_analyses.summary: The frontend container is a component‑based Angular application built with TypeScript, RxJS, HTML/CSS and Bootstrap, organized into presentation, service, adapter, and core/shared layers and consuming a rich set of REST APIs. It supports the full deed registry and workflow domain, exposing capabilities such as deed management, document processing, authentication, notifications, reporting, and bulk operations. Quality analysis shows moderate separation of concerns, coupling and cohesion, but a high technical debt due to an excess of services, components, pipes, and adapters, leading to a grade of C and several refactoring opportunities.

capabilities: Create, view, edit, approve and delete deeds
Manage deed workflows including archiving, deletion, reencryption and task handling
Import and process documents with metadata extraction and signature verification
Handle authentication and authorization (login, token management, guards)
Send and display notifications and alerts
Generate and present reports and analytics
Provide online help and contextual assistance
Manage numbers and identifiers within the system
Interact with external services via adapters (key management, secure framework, file storage, signature services)
Perform bulk data operations (bulk capture, bulk import)
Enforce rate limiting, busy indication and error handling for HTTP calls

debt_indicators: Excessive number of services (131) with versioned duplicates
High count of components (128) leading to potential oversized UI modules
Large number of pipes (67) indicating possible over‑fragmentation of presentation logic
Many adapters (50) without clear façade or abstraction layer
Potential mixing of business logic into services and adapters
recommendations: Consolidate version‑specific services into a single, configurable service or use strategy pattern
Introduce façade or domain‑service layers to decouple components from low‑level adapters
Refactor oversized components into smaller, reusable child components
Review and reduce the number of pipes; combine related transformations where possible
Group related services into feature‑specific modules to improve cohesion and discoverability
Document and enforce clear boundaries between presentation, service, and adapter layers

container_analyses.overall_assessment: The frontend is functionally comprehensive but suffers from structural bloat and moderate quality concerns; addressing service duplication, component size, and clearer layering will significantly improve maintainability and performance.
top_recommendations: Consolidate version‑specific services into a single configurable service or apply the strategy pattern
Introduce façade/domain‑service layers to isolate components from low‑level adapters
Refactor oversized components into smaller, reusable child components

executive_summary: uvz is a modular monolith system with 951 components across 2 active containers: backend (494 components, Grade C), frontend (404 components, Grade C). Overall architecture grade: C.
